返回本批次

在网络上安排任务

Schedule tasks on the web

code.claude.com 作者 iBelieve 分数 37 评论 21 2026/03/27 12:47
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. 计划任务使用 Anthropic 管理的基础设施以重复的节奏运行提示。即使计算机关闭，任务也能继续运行。您可以自动化重复工作的一些示例： 每天早上审查打开的拉取请求 分析夜间 CI 故障并显示摘要 在 PR 合并后同步文档 每周运行依赖项审计
  2. 计划任务可供 Web 上的所有 Claude Code 用户使用，包括 Pro、Max、Team 和 Enterprise。比较计划选项 Claude Code 提供三种计划重复工作的方法：CloudDesktop/loop 在人类云上运行您的机器您的机器需要机器开启NoYesYes需要打开会话NoNoYes在重新启动后持续存在YesYesNo（会话范围）访问本地文件No（新鲜克隆）YesYesMCP 服务器每个任务配置的连接器配置文件和连接器继承自会话权限提示否（自主运行）可按任务配置从会话继承可自定义计划通过 CLI 中的 /schedule 是是最小间隔 1 小时 1 分钟 1 分钟
  3. 创建计划任务 您可以从三个地方创建计划任务： Web：访问 claude.ai/code/scheduled，然后单击新建计划任务 桌面应用程序：打开计划页面，单击新建任务，然后选择新建远程任务。有关详细信息，请参阅桌面计划任务。 CLI：在任何会话中运行 /schedule。克劳德以对话方式引导您完成设置。您还可以直接传递描述，例如/安排每日上午 9 点进行 PR 审核。
  4. Web 和桌面入口点打开一个表单。 CLI 通过引导对话收集相同的信息。以下步骤将介绍 Web 界面。1234567 频率选项 日程选择器提供预设频率来为您处理时区转换。选择您本地区域的时间，任务就会按照挂钟时间运行，无论云基础设施位于何处。频率描述每小时运行一次。每日在您指定的时间每天运行一次。默认为当地时间上午 9:00。工作日与每日相同，但跳过周六和周日。每周在您指定的日期和时间运行一次。对于自定义间隔（例如每 2 小时或每个月的第一天），请选择最接近的预设并使用 /schedule update 从 CLI 更新计划以设置特定的计划。存储库和分支权限您添加的每个存储库都会在每次运行时克隆。除非您的提示另有指定，否则 Claude 从存储库的默认分支开始。默认情况下，Claude 只能推送到以 claude/ 为前缀的分支。这可以防止计划任务意外修改受保护或长期存在的分支。要删除特定存储库的此限制，请在创建或编辑任务时启用允许对该存储库进行不受限制的分支推送。 连接器 计划任务可以在每次运行期间使用连接的 MCP 连接器读取和写入外部服务。例如，对支持请求进行分类的任务可能会从 Slack 通道读取数据并在 Linear 中创建问题。创建任务时，默认情况下会包含当前连接的所有连接器。删除任何不需要限制 Claude 在运行期间可以访问的工具的内容。您还可以直接从任务表单添加连接器。要在任务表单之外管理或添加连接器，请访问 claude.ai 上的设置 > 连接器或使用 CLI.Environments 中的 /schedule update 每个任务都在控制网络访问、环境变量和设置脚本的云环境中运行。在创建任务之前配置环境，以授予 Claude 访问 API、安装依赖项或限制网络范围的权限。请参阅云环境以获取完整的设置指南。管理计划任务 单击计划列表中的任务以打开其详细信息页面。详细信息页面显示任务的存储库、连接器、提示、计划和过去运行的列表。查看运行并与运行交互单击任何运行将其作为完整会话打开。从那里您可以看到 Claude 做了什么、查看更改、创建拉取请求或继续对话。每个运行会话的工作方式与任何其他会话类似：使用会话标题旁边的下拉菜单对其进行重命名、存档或删除。 编辑和控制任务 在任务详细信息页面中，您可以： 单击“立即运行”立即开始运行，无需等待下一个计划时间。使用“重复”部分中的开关来暂停或恢复计划。暂停的任务会保留其配置，但在您重新启用它们之前不会运行。单击编辑图标可更改名称、提示、计划、存储库、环境或连接器。单击删除图标可删除任务。该任务创建的过去会话仍保留在您的会话列表中。
  5. 您还可以使用 /schedule 管理 CLI 中的任务。运行 /schedule list 以查看所有任务， /schedule update 以更改任务，或 /schedule run 以立即触发任务。

Original Summary

  1. A scheduled task runs a prompt on a recurring cadence using Anthropic-managed infrastructure. Tasks keep working even when your computer is off. A few examples of recurring work you can automate: Reviewing open pull requests each morning Analyzing CI failures overnight and surfacing summaries Syncing documentation after PRs merge Running dependency audits every week
  2. Scheduled tasks are available to all Claude Code on the web users, including Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise.Compare scheduling options Claude Code offers three ways to schedule recurring work:CloudDesktop/loopRuns onAnthropic cloudYour machineYour machineRequires machine onNoYesYesRequires open sessionNoNoYesPersistent across restartsYesYesNo (session-scoped)Access to local filesNo (fresh clone)YesYesMCP serversConnectors configured per taskConfig files and connectorsInherits from sessionPermission promptsNo (runs autonomously)Configurable per taskInherits from sessionCustomizable scheduleVia /schedule in the CLIYesYesMinimum interval1 hour1 minute1 minute
  3. Create a scheduled task You can create a scheduled task from three places: Web: visit claude.ai/code/scheduled and click New scheduled task Desktop app: open the Schedule page, click New task, and choose New remote task. See Desktop scheduled tasks for details. CLI: run /schedule in any session. Claude walks you through the setup conversationally. You can also pass a description directly, like /schedule daily PR review at 9am.
  4. The web and Desktop entry points open a form. The CLI collects the same information through a guided conversation. The steps below walk through the web interface.1234567 Frequency options The schedule picker offers preset frequencies that handle time zone conversion for you. Pick a time in your local zone and the task runs at that wall-clock time regardless of where the cloud infrastructure is located. FrequencyDescriptionHourlyRuns every hour.DailyRuns once per day at the time you specify. Defaults to 9:00 AM local time.WeekdaysSame as Daily but skips Saturday and Sunday.WeeklyRuns once per week on the day and time you specify. For custom intervals like every 2 hours or first of each month, pick the closest preset and update the schedule from the CLI with /schedule update to set a specific schedule.Repositories and branch permissions Each repository you add is cloned on every run. Claude starts from the repository’s default branch unless your prompt specifies otherwise. By default, Claude can only push to branches prefixed with claude/. This prevents scheduled tasks from accidentally modifying protected or long-lived branches. To remove this restriction for a specific repository, enable Allow unrestricted branch pushes for that repository when creating or editing the task.Connectors Scheduled tasks can use your connected MCP connectors to read from and write to external services during each run. For example, a task that triages support requests might read from a Slack channel and create issues in Linear. When you create a task, all of your currently connected connectors are included by default. Remove any that aren’t needed to limit which tools Claude has access to during the run. You can also add connectors directly from the task form. To manage or add connectors outside of the task form, visit Settings > Connectors on claude.ai or use /schedule update in the CLI.Environments Each task runs in a cloud environment that controls network access, environment variables, and setup scripts. Configure environments before creating a task to give Claude access to APIs, install dependencies, or restrict network scope. See cloud environment for the full setup guide.Manage scheduled tasks Click a task in the Scheduled list to open its detail page. The detail page shows the task’s repositories, connectors, prompt, schedule, and a list of past runs.View and interact with runs Click any run to open it as a full session. From there you can see what Claude did, review changes, create a pull request, or continue the conversation. Each run session works like any other session: use the dropdown menu next to the session title to rename, archive, or delete it.Edit and control tasks From the task detail page you can: Click Run now to start a run immediately without waiting for the next scheduled time. Use the toggle in the Repeats section to pause or resume the schedule. Paused tasks keep their configuration but don’t run until you re-enable them. Click the edit icon to change the name, prompt, schedule, repositories, environment, or connectors. Click the delete icon to remove the task. Past sessions created by the task remain in your session list.
  5. You can also manage tasks from the CLI with /schedule. Run /schedule list to see all tasks, /schedule update to change a task, or /schedule run to trigger one immediately.

评论（默认中文）

nickandbro 2026/03/27 13:13
我觉得我们正越来越接近一个默认软件快速迭代的世界。例如，受信任的用户做出反馈 -> 反馈由 AI 代理整理成票据，然后由代理转化为 PR，然后由代理审核，最后由代理部署。我们距离飞轮的完成可能还差一两步。或者也许我们已经在那里了。
I feel like we are just inching closer and closer to a world where rapid iteration of software will be by default. Like for example a trusted user makes feedback -> feedback gets curated into a ticket by an AI agent, then turned into a PR by an Agent, then reviewed by an Agent, before being deployed by an Agent. We are maybe one or two steps from the flywheel being completed. Or maybe we are already there.
在 HN 中查看
chatmasta 2026/03/27 13:36
我喜欢这个方向的一切，除了疯狂的推理成本。我不介意培训成本，因为模型一发布就商品化了。尽管我确实担心，如果推理成本下降，训练模型的公司将没有动力发布其权重，因为推理收入是他们收回训练成本的地方。

不管怎样……我们迫切需要在软件和硬件方面的推理性价比方面进行更多创新。如果软件创新能够开启对商品硬件的推理，那就太好了。这不太可能发生，但今天的尖端硬件就是明天的商品硬件，所以从某种意义上说，这也许会发生。

如果 Taalas 能够在两个月的时间内将模型烧录到硬件中，这将是巨大的进步，但仍然是浪费，因为那样我们就将问题转移到了硬件瓶颈上。我希望我们会看到类似于游戏机卡带的东西，它们生产成本低廉，并且可以插入基本模型以增强专业化。

但我也想知道是否有人正在追求一些更疯狂的激进想法，比如回归模拟计算并以巧妙的方式利用电压差。它对我来说太大了，但直观上感觉就像浪费熵来将电压尖峰降低到 0 或 1。

I love everything about this direction except for the insane inference costs. I don’t mind the training costs, since models are commoditized as soon as they’re released. Although I do worry that if inference costs drop, the companies training the models will have no incentive to publish their weights because inference revenue is where they recuperate the training cost.

Either way… we badly need more innovation in inference price per performance, on both the software and hardware side. It would be great if software innovation unlocked inference on commodity hardware. That’s unlikely to happen, but today’s bleeding edge hardware is tomorrow’s commodity hardware so maybe it will happen in some sense.

If Taalas can pull off burning models into hardware with a two month lead time, that will be huge progress, but still wasteful because then we’ve just shifted the problem to a hardware bottleneck. I expect we’ll see something akin to gameboy cartridges that are cheap to produce and can plug into base models to augment specialization.

But I also wonder if anyone is pursuing some more insanely radical ideas, like reverting back to analog computing and leveraging voltage differentials in clever ways. It’s too big brain for me, but intuitively it feels like wasting entropy to reduce a voltage spike to 0 or 1.

在 HN 中查看
tossandthrow 2026/03/27 13:33
我认为人工智能代理会直接做一个公关——门票是给智力有限的人类的。

至少在我的公司，我们离飞轮很近。

I think the Ai agent will directly make a PR - tickets are for humans with limited mental capacity.

At least in my company we are close to that flywheel.

在 HN 中查看
jvuygbbkuurx 2026/03/27 13:30
像贾坦这样值得信赖的用户。
Tusted user like Jia Tan.
在 HN 中查看
MattGaiser 2026/03/27 13:35
我已经和朋友一起进行了一个项目/初创公司。他在 GitHub 上写了一个问题，有一个工作会自动触发 Claude 尝试解决它并提交 PR。他可以看到短暂环境的变化。他还没有合并一个，但总有一天会合并到较小的项目。

我已经处于这样的境地，因为只有我们两个人，限制因素是他自己的需求，而不是我提供功能的能力。

I am already there with a project/startup with a friend. He writes up an issue in GitHub and there is a job that automatically triggers Claude to take a crack at it and throw up a PR. He can see the change in an ephemeral environment. He hasn't merged one yet, but it will get there one day for smaller items.

I am already at the point where because it is just the two of us, the limiting factor is his own needs, not my ability to ship features.

在 HN 中查看
yieldcrv 2026/03/27 13:34
我们自动反馈票证

我们没有任何类型的产品经理或技术票据撰写者

但我们的开发人员仍在选择如何处理票证，我们当然不必这样做，因为我正在用人工智能解决票证。如果我愿意，我可以自动化我的工作，但我不会相信结果，因为我提供了一定程度的输入和指导，并且有更大的考虑因素，目前它不擅长杂耍

We do feedback to ticket automatically

We dont have product managers or technical ticket writers of any sort

But us devs are still choosing how to tackle the ticket, we def don't have to as I’m solving the tickets with AI. I could automate my job away if I wanted, but I wouldn't trust the result as I give a degree of input and steering, and there’s bigger picture considerations its not good at juggling, for now

在 HN 中查看
charcircuit 2026/03/27 13:19
然后设置遥测并针对更改进行实验。然后，如果数据看起来不错，代理会将其提供给更多用户或将其删除。
Then sets up telemetry and experiments with the change. Then if data looks good an agent ramps it up to more users or removes it.
在 HN 中查看
gowthamgts12 2026/03/27 13:14
有趣的是，功能发布是通过官方网站发布的，而使用限制是通过团队成员的 Twitter 帐户发布的 - https://x.com/trq212/status/2037254607001559305。

另外，当他们宣布 2 倍使用时，有人正确地预测了这种拉力的出现 - https://x.com/Pranit/status/2033043924294439147

interesting to see feature launches are coming via official website while usage restrictions are coming in with a team member's twitter account - https://x.com/trq212/status/2037254607001559305.

also, someone rightly predicted this rugpull coming in when they announced 2x usage - https://x.com/Pranit/status/2033043924294439147

在 HN 中查看
stingraycharles 2026/03/27 13:16
对我来说，他们鼓励人们这样做是非常有意义的，而不是让每个人的东西变得更贵。

与根据一天中的时间收取不同的道路通行费相同。

To me it makes perfect sense for them to encourage people to do this, rather than eg making things more expensive for everyone.

The same as charging a different toll price on the road depending on the time of day.

在 HN 中查看
iBelieve 2026/03/27 13:18
看起来我只能执行 3 个云计划任务。我也参加了 Max 20x 计划:(

“您的计划每天有 3 个云计划会话。禁用或删除现有计划以继续。”

但除此之外，这看起来真的很酷。我尝试过在 Claude Code Desktop 和 Codex 桌面应用程序中使用本地计划任务，但很快就对权限提示感到恼火，因此能够在云沙箱中运行计划任务会很好。

以下是我将尝试的三项任务：

每个星期一早上：运行 `pnpm audit` 并研究任何安全问题，看看它们是否可能影响我们的项目。运行 `pnpm outdated` 并研究任何具有较小或主要升级可用的软件包。还要研究软件包是否已被废弃或很长时间没有更新，并查看是否有推荐的新替代方案。整理一份简短的报告，突出显示您的发现和建议。

每个工作日早上：查看过去几天的 Sentry 错误、日志和指标。查看是否出现任何新问题并进行调查。查看日志和指标，看看是否有任何异常，并进行适当的调查。整理一份总结所有发现的报告。

每个工作日早上：请查看前一天 `develop` 分支上的提交，仔细查看每个提交，看看是否有任何新引入的 bug、草率的代码、错过的功能、安全性差、缺少文档等。如果提交引用了 GitHub 问题，请查找该问题，并审查该问题以查看该提交是否正确实现了票证（全部或部分）。还要扫描代码库，寻找容易实现的目标，这些任务可能是建议委托给 AI 代理的好任务：明显的错误、糟糕或不正确的文档、TODO 注释、混乱的代码、小的改进等。

我刚才将所有这些都作为一次性任务运行，它们将有用的报告放在一起；每天/每周收到这些会很高兴。 Claude Code 有一个可在其云/Web 环境中工作的 Sentry 连接器。这很酷;它准确地识别了我本周一直在解决的问题。

我最终可能会尝试让这些任务打开问题，甚至自动解决问题并打开 PR，但我们现在只从报告开始。

Looks like I'm limited to only 3 cloud scheduled tasks. And I'm on the Max 20x plan, too :(

"Your plan gets 3 daily cloud scheduled sessions. Disable or delete an existing schedule to continue."

But otherwise, this looks really cool. I've tried using local scheduled tasks in both Claude Code Desktop and the Codex desktop app, and very quickly got annoyed with permissions prompts, so it'll be nice to be able to run scheduled tasks in the cloud sandbox.

Here are the three tasks I'll be trying:

Every Monday morning: Run `pnpm audit` and research any security issues to see if they might affect our project. Run `pnpm outdated` and research into any packages with minor or major upgrades available. Also research if packages have been abandoned or haven't been updated in a long time, and see if there are new alternatives that are recommended instead. Put together a brief report highlighting your findings and recommendations.

Every weekday morning: Take at Sentry errors, logs, and metrics for the past few days. See if there's any new issues that have popped up, and investigate them. Take a look at logs and metrics, and see if anything seems out of the ordinary, and investigate as appropriate. Put together a report summarizing any findings.

Every weekday morning: Please look at the commits on the `develop` branch from the previous day, look carefully at each commit, and see if there are any newly introduced bugs, sloppy code, missed functionality, poor security, missing documentation, etc. If a commit references GitHub issues, look up the issue, and review the issue to see if the commit correctly implements the ticket (fully or partially). Also do a sweep through the codebase, looking for low-hanging fruit that might be good tasks to recommend delegating to an AI agent: obvious bugs, poor or incorrect documentation, TODO comments, messy code, small improvements, etc.

I ran all of these as one-off tasks just now, and they put together useful reports; it'll be nice getting these on a daily/weekly basis. Claude Code has a Sentry connector that works in their cloud/web environment. That's cool; it accurately identified an issue I've been working on this week.

I might eventually try having these tasks open issues or even automatically address issues and open PRs, but we'll start with just reports for now.

0 7 * * 1-5 ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-... /path/to/claude-cron.sh /path/to/repo >> ~/claude-reports.md 2>&1

看起来微不足道。

0 7 * * 1-5 ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-... /path/to/claude-cron.sh /path/to/repo >> ~/claude-reports.md 2>&1

Seems trivial.

This is powerful. Combined with MCPs, you can pretty much automate a ton of work.
在 HN 中查看
zmmmmm 2026/03/27 13:29
我在这里缺少一些基本的东西......它实际上是做什么的？它针对 git 存储库执行提示。好吧——但是然后呢？输出去哪儿了？无论这个提示的结果是什么，它实际上是如何持续存在的？

这是假设你给了它 git commit 权限并且它就这样做了吗？或者它通过您启用的 MCP 工具起作用？

i'm missing something basic here .... what does it actually do? It executes a prompt against a git repository. Fine - but then what? Where does the output go? How does it actually persist whatever the outcome of this prompt is?

Is this assuming you give it git commit permission and it just does that? Or it acts through MCP tools you enable?

jngiam1 2026/03/27 13:38

MCP tools. We're doing some MCP bundling and giving it here, pretty cool stuff.

tossandthrow 2026/03/27 13:30
我们每周都会对代码库进行自动安全审计，并将结果发布在 Slack 上
We use to do do automated sec audits weekly on the code base and post the result on slack
在 HN 中查看
zmmmmm 2026/03/27 13:31
那么 Slack Posting 是它拥有的 MCP 工具吗？还是它只知道的技能？
so is slack posting an MCP tool it has? or a skill it just knows?
在 HN 中查看
tossandthrow 2026/03/27 13:35
在 Claude 中，它是一个“连接器”，本质上是一个 mcp 工具。
In Claude it is a "connector" which is essentially an mcp tool.

arjie 2026/03/27 13:05

What's the per-unit-time compute cost (independent of tokens)? Compute deadline etc.? They don't charge for the Cloud Environment https://code.claude.com/docs/en/claude-code-on-the-web#cloud... currently running?
